L1.area: External service connection management and remote execution infrastructure

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where a shared connection instance is reused across multiple users in a multi-user remote execution context. The connection, once established by one user, is served to all subsequent users without re-authentication, allowing any user to access data using another user's credentials.

L3.area: MISP API client initialization and connection lifecycle management in the remote transform subsystem
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where a global singleton is used to cache and share a threat intelligence platform API connection across all users in a remote transform execution context. Once one user establishes a connection with their API credentials, all subsequent users receive the same authenticated connection, allowing unauthorized access to the first user's data and bypassing per-user authentication.

ground_truth.reason: The application uses a global singleton to cache the MISP API connection, causing it to be shared across all users in a remote-transform deployment. This means once one user authenticates, all subsequent users bypass authentication and use the first user's credentials.
